text: I am not sad at all
notes: Simple negation flips sadness to joy

text: I'm not happy about the results
notes: Simple negation flips joy to sadness

text: I don't feel stressed today
I never feel lonely when I'm with them
I can't find any joy in things
Nothing makes me excited anymore
I'm not angry, just disappointed
I don't hate my job

notes: Haven't modifies timeline, not the emotion itself

text: I can't believe they didn't apologize
I can't stop worrying about the exam
notes: Can't stop = continuous, not negation of worry

text: I won't tolerate this disrespect
I'm not going to let them get away
Nothing is bothering me today
I'm not as stressed as I thought I'd be
I didn't deserve to be treated like that
notes: Didn't modifies deserve, emotion is anger

text: I'm not okay with how they handled it
Had a good lunch but work was stressful
notes: Clause boundary: 'but' separates positive from negative

text: I was happy at first but then everything fell apart

She was kind to me however I still felt uneasy
I love my job although the commute kills me
The movie was great yet I came home feeling sad
I'm not sad, I'm furious
notes: Not sad negation, but furious in second clause

text: I'm not tired, I'm exhausted
notes: Not tired negation, but exhausted amplifies
